diagrams.description: This timeline traces the historical evolution of Behavioral Economics from early psychological economics to modern applications. Key milestones include: early roots in the 1890s-1940s (Adam Smith's moral sentiments, Irving Fisher's utility, George Katona); the emergence in 1955 with Herbert Simon's bounded rationality; 1974 Tversky & Kahneman's heuristics and biases; 1979 Prospect Theory; 1980s Thaler's mental accounting and framing; 1990s behavioral finance (Shiller, Thaler); 2000s policy applications with nudge and the Behavioural Insights Team; and culminating in the 2017 Nobel Prize for Thaler. Recent advances extend into AI and health.
